title: --sudo option: 6-layer elevation architecture with pulse tracing (v5.14)
desc: --sudo is a SESSION START trigger. While admin Eye (pipe: wkappbot_elevated) is alive, subsequent commands should route through it and inherit admin privilege — like shell sudo. Architecture: Core/SudoHandler = single owner for spawning admin Eye (Ping pre-check, hot-swap-if-needed, UAC, post-UAC 100ms handshake). User Eye (pipe: WKAppBotCmdPipe) refuses --sudo with [EYE:FALLBACK] + exit 125. Pipe names are FIXED: admin=wkappbot_elevated, user=WKAppBotCmdPipe. IsAvailable→Ping migration for liveness.
steps: SEMANTIC MODEL — --sudo is a SESSION START, not per-invocation. Once admin Eye (pipe wkappbot_elevated) is up, subsequent commands should inherit admin privilege without re-typing --sudo. Like shell sudo. This means Launcher should prefer admin pipe over user pipe when admin Eye is alive.

TO END ADMIN SESSION — kill admin Eye via a11y kill. There is NO --no-sudo flag; the session lifetime is the admin Eye process lifetime. Once admin Eye exits, commands fall back to user Eye / user privilege automatically.

PIPE NAME INVARIANTS — NEVER rename these. Admin Eye: \.\pipe\wkappbot_elevated (served by ElevatedEyeServer). User Eye: \.\pipe\WKAppBotCmdPipe (served by EyeCmdPipeServer). Both exist simultaneously when both Eyes run. Changing either breaks every downstream caller, PS scripts, and tests.
LAYER 1 — MCP Launcher pre-execution gate (McpProxy.cs). Detects --sudo in JSON-RPC requests; pre-spawns admin Core.
LAYER 3 — eye --sudo uses SudoHandler.EnsureAdminForSudo side-by-side (non-destructive). Old user Eye preserved.
LAYER 4 — Eye auto-launch --sudo inheritance (AppBotEyeCommands.cs): Program.IsElevated()==true appends --sudo marker so respawned Eyes stay elevated.
LAYER 5 — Hot-swap is RENAME ONLY. SudoHandler.PromoteNewExeIfPending never spawns Eye. Separation of concerns: file rename != process launch.
LAYER 6 — Launcher-side --sudo bypass + stderr passthrough. isSudoRequest skips user Eye pipe delegation. showStderr implicit true when --sudo so PULSE trail visible.

LAYER 7 — User Eye refuses --sudo via pipe with FALLBACK exit 125 (EyeCmdPipeServer checks args.Contains(--sudo) BEFORE dispatch, commit 9220f66). Prevents silent wrong-scope execution if a caller mistakenly routes --sudo through user pipe.

LAYER 8 — LaunchElevatedEye / SudoHandler.SpawnAndAwaitAdmin does a post-UAC 100ms Ping handshake. UAC dialog delay is free wait time for pre-booting admin Eye to finish. If responsive → reuse and skip our spawn. Prevents the broadcast-close churn that killed in-flight requests (weeks of regressions).

LAYER 9 — IsAvailable → Ping migration (commit 4664def). IsAvailable checks file presence; pipe name can outlive crashed server. Ping(100) does ConnectAsync — authoritative liveness. Sites: Program.cs eye --sudo, ElevatedEyeProxy.cs lines 405/452/484, SudoHandler pre-check.

LAYER 10 (PLANNED) — Launcher pipe selection by admin Eye liveness. Before delegating to user pipe, Ping admin pipe (100ms). If alive → route through admin pipe (inherits admin privilege). If dead → user pipe (current behavior).

ADMIN EYE SELF-SWAP REMOVED (commit c0bbf27). AppBotEyeCommands.cs no longer runs FSW on .new.exe / .exe. Old dual-FSW caused infinite successor loops when .new.exe lingered. All admin Eye lifecycle decisions flow through SudoHandler alone.

SudoHandler.EnsureAdminForSudo flow: (1) Ping admin Eye 100ms, alive → reuse no-op. (2) PromoteNewExeIfPending (rename only). (3) runas StartTracked + UAC. (4) Post-UAC 100ms handshake (Layer 8). (5) Poll admin pipe up to 10s for our spawn.

SudoHandler failure path: return false → Program.cs emits [SUDO:FALLBACK] markers → normal dispatch continues as regular user. Admin-only targets fail with Access Denied. No hard exit; visibility over silence.

DEBUGGING CHECKLIST. wkappbot --sudo <cmd> 2>&1 — inspect PULSE, [SUDO:FALLBACK], [SUDO:HOT-SWAP] markers. tasklist for cores. Ping both pipes via PowerShell NamedPipeClientStream.Connect(100). Run tests/sudo/test-mcp-sudo-gate.sh + test-sudo-stress.sh.
RESOLVED: windows cmd 30s hang on restricted process (commit bd26a28). GetProcessPerf uses 300ms worker-thread timeout on Process.GetProcessById property access.
RESOLVED: --sudo fast-fail budget 1.5s → 3s (commit 13dbf3b) — tight budget tripped healthy admin Eye and caused recovery-spawn + broadcast-close churn.
author: claude
